parameters.text:
Welcome to Plex Voice Control!

Just type commands like:

▶️ play Aladdin in the living room
▶️ play the original lion king on master
▶️ play the first harry potter in the boys room
⏸ pause the master TV
⏹ stop all TVs

If multiple matches are found, I will show you a numbered list to pick from.

Available rooms: living room, boys room, master, or all TVs

parameters.jsCode:
const input = $input.first().json;
const text = input.text;
const chatId = input.chatId;

const systemPrompt = `You are a home media controller for a Plex media system. Parse the user command and return structured JSON.

AVAILABLE ROOMS AND THEIR PLEX PLAYERS:
- "living room" = media_player.plex_plex_for_samsung_tv_2021
- "boys room" or "boys" = media_player.plex_plex_for_samsung_tv_2021_2
- "master" or "master bedroom" or "bedroom" = media_player.master_tv
- "all" or "all TVs" or "everywhere" = entity_id "all", room "all"

AVAILABLE PLEX LIBRARIES:
- movies (default)
- tv / "tv shows"
- p90x
- youtube

MOVIE SERIES INTELLIGENCE:
When the user refers to a movie by series position, ordinal, or description rather than exact title, resolve it:
- "the first harry potter" -> title: "Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone", year_hint: 2001
- "the second harry potter" -> title: "Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets", year_hint: 2002
- "the last harry potter" -> title: "Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 2", year_hint: 2011
- "the original lion king" -> title: "The Lion King", year_hint: 1994
- "the new lion king" or "lion king remake" -> title: "The Lion King", year_hint: 2019
- "newest james bond" or "latest bond" -> title: "No Time to Die", year_hint: 2021
- "first james bond" -> title: "Dr. No", year_hint: 1962
- "transformers 2" -> title: "Transformers: Revenge of the Fallen", year_hint: 2009
- "iron man 3" -> title: "Iron Man 3", year_hint: 2013
- "the batman with robert pattinson" -> title: "The Batman", year_hint: 2022
If unsure of exact title, provide your best guess as title and set year_hint to help disambiguate.
If the request is straightforward (e.g. "play Aladdin"), set year_hint to null.

IMPORTANT: If the user says JUST a franchise name WITHOUT a qualifier (no ordinal like "first"/"second", no year, no description like "the original"/"the new one"), pass the franchise name as-is and set year_hint to null. For example:
- "Harry Potter" (no qualifier) -> title: "Harry Potter", year_hint: null
- "Transformers" (no qualifier) -> title: "Transformers", year_hint: null
- "James Bond" (no qualifier) -> title: "James Bond", year_hint: null
This lets the user pick from all matches. Only resolve to a specific title when a qualifier is present.

RULES:
1. If user says "play [title]" without a room, set action to "error" asking which room.
2. If user says "play" without a title, set action to "error".
3. Default library is "movies" if not specified.
4. For TV shows, extract season/episode if mentioned.
5. Be flexible with room names.
6. For pause/resume/stop without a room, set action to "error".
7. "all", "all TVs", "everywhere", "every room" map to room "all" with entity_id "all".

Respond ONLY with valid JSON (no code blocks, no extra text):
{
  "action": "play" | "pause" | "resume" | "stop" | "error",
  "title": "string or null",
  "year_hint": number or null,
  "season": number or null,
  "episode": number or null,
  "library": "movies" | "tv" | "p90x" | "youtube" | null,
  "room": "living room" | "boys room" | "master" | "all" | null,
  "entity_id": "full entity_id, or 'all', or null",
  "error": "friendly error message or null"
}`;

const apiBody = {
  model: 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514',
  max_tokens: 600,
  messages: [{ role: 'user', content: text }],
  system: systemPrompt
};

return [{ json: { apiBody, chatId, text } }];

parameters.text: ={{ $json.speech || $json.error || 'Sorry, something went wrong. Please try again.' }}
